channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: About 8 in 10 say they've recently experienced extreme weather: Survey
item.description: Overall, nearly 70 percent of adults believe in climate change, with 93 percent of Democrats and 51 percent of Republicans saying so, poll results show.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge extends block on Trump gender-affirming care federal funding ban
item.description: U.S. District Court Judge Lauren King ruled that President Trump’s executive orders violate the Constitution’s Fifth Amendment, which grants equal protection under the law.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cuomo jumps into New York mayor's race, attempting political comeback
item.description: Former New York Gov. Andrew Cuomo (D) announced Saturday afternoon that he's entering New York City’s mayoral race, adding a big name to the already busy Democratic primary challenging Mayor Eric Adams (D).
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, Fox 5

item.title: Threatening letter sent to northern Colorado family over MAGA banner
item.description: A Severance family says they recently received a threatening letter in the mail, in response to a "Make America Great Again" banner posted in their front yard.
item.dc:creator: Greg Nieto, NewsNation

item.title: Singer Angie Stone killed in car crash after leaving Alabama performance
item.description: Stone was riding in the back of a vehicle that "flipped over and subsequently hit by a big rig," according to family and friends.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Ratliff, KREX 5

item.title: Grammy-nominated R&B singer Angie Stone dies in car crash
item.description: Grammy-nominated R&B singer Angie Stone, a member of the all-female hip-hop trio The Sequence and known for the hit song “Wish I Didn’t Miss You,” was killed early Saturday in a car crash.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Singer Angie Stone killed in car crash after performance: Report
item.description: TMZ says per Stone's representative, Stone left a performance in Alabama early Saturday when she was killed in a car crash.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Ratliff, NewsNation

item.title: David Johansen, singer from the seminal punk band the New York Dolls, dies at 75
item.description: David Johansen, the wiry, gravelly-voiced singer and last surviving member of the glam and protopunk band the New York Dolls who later performed as his campy, pompadoured alter ego, Buster Poindexter, has died. He was 75.
item.dc:creator: Mark Kennedy, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The world's most famous sled dog race is longer than ever. Here's a look at Iditarod, by the numbers
item.description: Thanks to a lack of snow, this year's edition of the Iditarod Trail Sled Dog Race needed a new route across the Alaska wilderness. The course change adds more than 100 miles (161 kilometers), so teams of dogs will have to pull their mushers over 1,100 miles (1,770 kilometers) to the finish line.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US kills senior military leader of Al-Qaeda affiliate in Syria
item.description: U.S. Central Command announced on Saturday that it had killed a senior military leader of an Al-Qaeda affiliate known as Hurras al-Din in an airstrike in Syria.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Border czar makes the city of Boston his latest immigration target
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — As Mayor Michelle Wu prepares to go to Congress to defend Boston's protections of immigrant communities, the city is in a war of words with the Trump administration and Republican representatives over how much police should support deportations.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WATCH: 2 window washers rescued from 'spinning' scaffolding in New York City
item.description: The wind took over a scaffolding while two workers were washing the windows of a high-rise building in Midtown.
item.dc:creator: Eliecer Marte, ABC 4

item.title: 2 window washers rescued from 'spinning' scaffolding in New York City
item.description: The wind took over a scaffolding while two workers were washing the windows of a high-rise building in Midtown in New York.
item.dc:creator: Eliecer Marte, NewsNation

item.title: Video shows implosion of iconic Six Flags rollercoaster Kingda Ka
item.description: The king has fallen. The legendary Kingda Ka ride at Six Flags Great Adventure in New Jersey was imploded on Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, ABC 4

item.title: Trump cutoff of humanitarian parole for immigrants from Ukraine, 6 other countries challenged
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — A group of American citizens and immigrants is suing the Trump administration for ending a long-standing legal tool presidents have used to allow people from countries where there's war or political instability to enter and temporarily live in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox Hollow serial killer's secret tapes could reveal murder horrors if ever found: documentary
item.description: Herb Baumeister, a thrift store owner, is believed to have killed at least 25 people from the 1980s to the 1990s and buried them throughout his $1 million, 18-acre property.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New York's governor orders college to remove Palestinian studies job listing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York's governor ordered a state school to remove a job posting for a Palestinian studies teaching position this week, saying she wanted to ensure “antisemitic theories” would not be taught.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Peak bloom for the iconic cherry blossom trees of Washington, DC, is predicted for late March
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — For decades, the thousands of blooming cherry blossom trees in the nation’s capital have served as both a magnet to visitors and the unofficial start of the tourist season for Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Actor Gene Hackman's death not from carbon monoxide, authorities say
item.description: Authorities investigating the deaths of legendary actor Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa say they have ruled out carbon monoxide poisoning as the cause.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Zelenskyy thanks Trump for visit after Oval Office clash
item.description: The meeting escalated after Vice President JD Vance suggested a diplomatic solution could be reached with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: Friday’s 'Planetary Parade' will give you a rare view of the Milky Way
item.description: With the stresses on planet Earth in the headlines, at work and your day-to-day life, do you need a reminder that we're just a speck on a dot in the endless vacuum of space? If so, today's your day.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Patrolling America's 'third border' from the skies
item.description: The U.S. Coast Guard is taking to the skies to monitor Florida's coast as a crackdown on illegal immigration continues.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Watch the full Trump-Zelenskyy Oval Office meeting
item.description: President Donald Trump shouted at Ukraine's leader on Friday during an extraordinary meeting in the Oval Office, berating President Volodymyr Zelenskyy for “gambling with millions of lives” and suggesting his actions could trigger World War III.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tate brothers, who face human trafficking charges in Romania, are back in the US: What to know
item.description: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate, who are charged with human trafficking in Romania, arrived Thursday in the U.S. after authorities lifted travel restrictions on the siblings, who have millions of online followers.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KRON4

item.title: Pope Francis suffers medical setback, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis suffered a bronchial spasm on Friday that resulted in him breathing in vomit, requiring non-invasive mechanical ventilation, the Vatican said in relaying a setback in his two-week long battle against double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Washington man arrested for stealing $8M fishing boat with crew still on board, officials say
item.description: The suspect allegedly tried to steal a different boat earlier that morning, but its crew "ran him off the boat," police said.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, WGN 9

item.title: Trump says he plans to add White House ballroom
item.description: President Trump in a new interview said he is planning to pave part of the Rose Garden, similar to a patio at his Mar-a-Lago property in Florida.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis continues to improve but his prognosis remains guarded: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis continued his slow recovery from double pneumonia on Thursday, beating back speculation of an imminent death, resignation or conclave and signalling that he was still very much in charge albeit in a weakened state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gene Hackman, wife Betsy Arakawa were dead for some time, found in different rooms, investigators say
item.description: A spokesperson for the state’s medical examiner declined to comment on whether any conclusions had been reached on the cause.
item.dc:creator: Susan Montoya Bryan, ABC 4

item.title: Anti-Israel protesters at Barnard College arrested after mob turns violent
item.description: Protests continued another day after a group of more than 50 anti-Israel protesters took over a building at New York City's Barnard College on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Savage timeline in Princeton preppy murder could bolster insanity defense for suspect brother: expert
item.description: The Princeton, New Jersey, man known as a star student in high school who is now accused of killing his brother likely had a mental breakdown, an expert said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Deal reached to end illegal strike by New York state prison workers, governor says
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York Gov. Kathy Hochul announced an agreement late Thursday to end a wildcat strike that has roiled the state’s prison system for more than a week. Hochul said the state and the union for striking correctional workers agreed to binding terms after four days of mediation talks.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NOAA begins firing hundreds of staffers
item.description: The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) began the process of firing hundreds of probationary workers Thursday, the latest mass job cuts within federal agencies, three sources with knowledge of internal operations told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Elon Musk urges retired air traffic controllers to come back
item.description: Earlier this month, President Trump’s administration began firing hundreds of workers at the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA), including personnel brought on for the FAA radar as well as landing and navigational aid maintenance.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, ABC 4

item.title: A look at Louisiana's Mardi Gras festivities through the years
item.description: After a weekslong pre-Lenten bash — complete with elaborate parades that meander through New Orleans, shimmery beads tossed from floats, streets lined with costumed revelers, lavish balls and seafood boils — Louisiana's 2025 Carnival Season is approaching its grand conclusion.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gayle King, Katy Perry, Lauren Sánchez to board 'historic' Blue Origin space flight
item.description: "CBS Mornings" co-anchor Gayle King, singer Katy Perry and Lauren Sánchez will be among an all-woman crew blasting off on Jeff Bezos's Blue Origin as part of what's being called an "historic" space flight.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Oscar-winner Gene Hackman, his wife and their dog found dead in their New Mexico home
item.description: The gruff-but-beloved Hackman was among the finest actors of his generation, appearing as both villains, heroes and antiheroes in dozens of dramas, comedies and action films from the 1960s until his retirement in the early 2000s.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
